name: La Litera. BTT. Ruta 2: Circular de Baldellou y Camporrells
name_original: Congosto de Baldellou y Coll de Baldellou pasando por Camporells— circular
description: This difficult 18.0-mile touring cycling route through Congosto de Baldellou offers varied terrain, significant climbs, and rich natural sce
region_intro: Touring cycling around Baldellou offers diverse landscapes, characterized by the tranquil Baldellou Reservoir and the striking Congosto de Baldellou gorge. The region features varied terrain, from open skies to fragrant pine trees, with views of the Montsec mountain range. Its semi-arid climate and limestone bedrock contribute to a unique cycling environment. This area provides an extensive network of back roads suitable for touring cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Baldellou The most popular touring cycling route is Montsec Bikepacking Loop 2 - Montsec d'Estall, a difficult 26.3 miles (42.2 km) trail that takes 3 hours 53 minutes to complete. This route features significant elevation gain through the Montsec d'Estall area. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is La Litera. BTT. Ruta 2: Circular de Baldellou y Camporrells, a difficult 17.9 miles (28.9 km) path. This route offers varied terrain, including sections around Baldellou and Camporrells. Local touring cyclists also love the Os de Balaguer loop from Ivars de Noguera, a 28.9 miles (46.5 km) trail leading through the area surrounding Os de Balaguer, often completed in about 4 hours 14 minutes. Touring cycling around Baldellou is defined by the Baldellou Reservoir, the Congosto de Baldellou gorge, and views of the Montsec mountain range. The network offers options for different ability levels, with 2 moderate and 11 difficult routes available. The routes in Baldellou are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 1 review. More than 60 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Baldellou's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Baldellou?
A: There are 13 touring cycling routes around Baldellou. These routes offer a variety of experiences, with 2 classified as moderate and 11 as difficult, catering to different skill levels.

Q: What natural landmarks or scenic views can I expect to see while cycling around Baldellou?
A: While touring cycling around Baldellou, you can expect breathtaking views of the Montsec mountain range, including Montsec d'Ares and Montsec de Rubies. Key natural features include the tranquil Baldellou Reservoir (also known as Santa Ana Reservoir) and the striking &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2450973" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Congosto de Baldellou&lt;/a&gt; gorge. You might also encounter the gigantic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3954955" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canelles Dam&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the Baldellou area?
A: Yes, several routes are designed as loops. For example, you can explore &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1360099021/la-litera-btt-ruta-2-circular-de-baldellou-y-camporrells" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Litera. BTT. Ruta 2: Circular de Baldellou y Camporrells&lt;/a&gt;, which offers varied terrain. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10467285" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baldellou Reservoir – Baldellou Gorge loop from Ivars de Noguera&lt;/a&gt;, a challenging ride through prominent natural attractions.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Baldellou?
A: Baldellou's semi-arid climate makes it favorable for cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for touring cycling, avoiding the peak heat of summer and potential chill of winter. The region's open skies and fragrant pine trees enhance the experience during these seasons.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling routes suitable for all ages in Baldellou?
A: While many routes in Baldellou are classified as difficult, there are a couple of moderate options that might be more suitable for families or those seeking a less strenuous ride. One such route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6954884" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Santo Toribio loop from Baélls&lt;/a&gt;, which is often on mostly paved surfaces.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about their experience in Baldellou?
A: The touring cycling routes in Baldellou are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Over 60 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the direct contact with nature and the diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural points of interest accessible from the cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region offers cultural highlights. For instance, the area around Os de Balaguer, which can be explored via the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38174557" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Os de Balaguer loop from Ivars de Noguera&lt;/a&gt;, boasts an Arab-origin castle and the monastery of Bellpuig. You can also find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4446234" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Finestras Abandoned Village&lt;/a&gt; and various hermitages like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1621342" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of the Mare de Déu de Cérvoles&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3011545" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Salvador Hermitage&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is there parking available for cyclists near the touring routes in Baldellou?
A: While specific parking areas for each route are not detailed, Baldellou and surrounding villages typically offer public parking options. It's advisable to check local parking availability in towns like Ivars de Noguera or Baélls, which serve as starting points for several routes.

Q: How can I access the touring cycling routes around Baldellou using public transport?
A: Public transport options directly to the start of specific touring cycling routes in Baldellou may be limited. The region is part of rural Aragon, so accessing trailheads often requires personal vehicle transport. However, larger towns in the Huesca province may have bus connections, from which you might need to cycle to your desired starting point.

Q: Are there places to eat or stay near the touring cycling routes in Baldellou?
A: Baldellou and its surrounding villages, such as Camporrells, Baélls, or Ivars de Noguera, offer local amenities including cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options like guesthouses or rural hotels. These can provide convenient stops for refreshments or overnight stays during your touring cycling adventure.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the touring cycling routes around Baldellou?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many outdoor trails in the Baldellou region, especially on less crowded routes. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or wildlife, and to check for any specific local regulations or protected areas that might have restrictions.

Q: What is the most challenging touring cycling route in Baldellou?
A: Among the difficult routes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1360085463/montsec-bikepacking-loop-2-montsec-d-estall" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montsec Bikepacking Loop 2 - Montsec d'Estall&lt;/a&gt; stands out with significant elevation gain over its 42.2 km, appealing to experienced riders seeking a demanding ride through the Montsec d'Estall area.
tips: The Pedriza river digs a congosto of short length but with a special charm. There is an artificial swamp and a couple of waterfalls where you can enjoy the views. Right next to the river there is a path that allows you to see the congosto up close, this path leaves Baldellou. The views from the road are spectacular and well deserve a stop. In summer it is a small oasis where to hide from the typical heat of the area.

This hermitage is dedicated to the patron saint of nearby Baells. There, an active neighborhood association with the name of the Saint, wove a huge mural with strips of recycled plastic using the crochet technique and with it they decorate the hermitage during the patron saint festivities.

Progressive climb without large percentages that becomes very bearable and allows you to enjoy the views. Asphalt in good condition and with very little traffic of vehicles (as in the whole area).

faqs.question: How difficult is the Congosto de Baldellou and Coll de Baldellou passing through Camporells— circular route for cycling?
faqs.answer: This route is considered difficult for touring cyclists, requiring very good fitness. It features significant elevation gain and some challenging sections where you might need to push your bike.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is primarily paved, but cyclists should be prepared for some unpaved segments that can be difficult to ride. In certain challenging parts, it may even be necessary to push your bike.

faqs.question: What natural features and wildlife can I expect to see along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll cycle through the picturesque Baldellou Gorge, known for its imposing limestone walls and rich botanical diversity, including pine trees, holm oaks, and wild olive trees. Birdwatching is excellent, with species like woodpeckers, griffon vultures, and wallcreepers. You'll also pass the Baldellou Reservoir, home to diverse dragonfly and damselfly populations.

faqs.question: Are there any specific points of interest or landmarks on the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, notable points include the stunning Baldellou Gorge, the historic Hermitage of Santo Toribio, and the scenic Coll de Baldellou. The route also passes through the charming village of Baldellou with its well-preserved medieval streets.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The trail is best enjoyed during seasons with moderate temperatures and stable weather conditions, typically spring and autumn, to make the most of the varied terrain and natural beauty.

faqs.question: Is the Congosto de Baldellou and Coll de Baldellou passing through Camporells— circular route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Information regarding dog-friendliness for cycling on this specific route is not explicitly provided. It's advisable to check local regulations or contact authorities if you plan to bring a dog, especially given the varied terrain and potential for wildlife encounters.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay any entrance fees to cycle this trail?
faqs.answer: There is no information indicating that permits or entrance fees are required to access this cycling route.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Congosto de Baldellou and Coll de Baldellou circular route?
faqs.answer: Specific parking information for the trailhead is not detailed. However, as a circular route passing through Camporells and the village of Baldellou, you may find parking options within or near these areas to begin your ride.

faqs.question: Does this cycling route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other trails, including La Litera. BTT. Ruta 2. Yesos y Embalse de Santa Ana, Senderos de La Litera Alta, and La Litera. BTT. Ruta 1. Sierras de La Litera. Smaller portions also overlap with Camporrells - Estopiñán, Camporells - Baldellou, and Cabañera de la Rebollosa.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginner touring cyclists?
faqs.answer: No, this route is categorized as 'difficult' and requires 'very good fitness' due to its significant elevation gain and challenging terrain, including unpaved sections where you might need to push your bike. It is not recommended for beginner touring cyclists.

faqs.question: What kind of bicycle is recommended for this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the mix of paved and unpaved, potentially difficult sections, a robust touring bicycle or a mountain bike capable of handling diverse terrain would be most suitable for this route.
